# sourcevision

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 142
- **Lines of code**: 46,136
- **Languages**: TypeScript (129), JSON (7), Markdown (3), Other (3)
- **Zones**: 9 (9 with descriptions)
- **Git**: main @ 63d150a
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-08T23:56:41.193Z

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- God function: generateLlmsTxt in src/analyzers/llms-txt.ts calls 33 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- Dead-export candidates (findings 13, 14: detectBranchLifecycle and resolveWorkedEpicTitlesForRange) both live at zone boundary interfaces — one in branch-work-analysis, one in cli-operations. Unused exports at zone boundaries are higher risk than internal dead code because they represent API surface that may attract new dependents before being removed. Verify and delete if unused rather than leaving them as latent attachment points. [info]
- The generateLlmsTxt god function (33 outgoing calls, finding 15) and the src/cli/output.ts fan-in hub (13 incoming files, finding 3) represent opposite structural extremes in the same codebase — one module that does too much, one that is depended on by too many. Neither alone is critical, but together they indicate that complexity is concentrating at I/O boundaries (output formatting and file generation) rather than distributing across domain logic. [info]

### Observations

- Isolated files — no import edges between 2 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (.claude)
- 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (analyzers)
- zones.ts is imported by 4 different zones (finding 1) and resides inside the same undifferentiated src/analyzers/ directory. Its hub status combined with ambiguous placement means consumers cannot tell whether they are importing a shared utility or violating a zone boundary. [info] (analyzers)
- Generic zone name "Analyzers 2" — enrichment did not assign a meaningful name reflecting this zone's domain purpose [warning] (analyzers-2)
- High coupling (0.52) — 15 imports target "analyzers" [warning] (analyzers-2)
- Generic zone name "Analyzers 3" — enrichment did not assign a meaningful name reflecting this zone's domain purpose [warning] (analyzers-3)
- High coupling (0.71) — 11 imports target "analyzers" [warning] (analyzers-3)
- Low cohesion (0.29) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (analyzers-3)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (fixtures)
- ... and 7 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "analyzers" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (analyzers)
- Create subdirectories inside src/analyzers/ that mirror the five logical zones whose files currently co-reside there (analysis-engine, branch-work-analysis, cli-operations, file-analysis-pipeline, zone-detection-engine). Move files without changing exports — this is a mechanical rename, not a refactor. Subdirectory names make zone membership visible to new contributors without requiring zone metadata lookups. [warning] (analyzers)
- Zone "cli" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli)
- Zone "fixtures" has files across 5 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (fixtures)
- src/analyzers/ contains files from 5 zones (analyzers, analyzers-2, analyzers-3, cli, unit) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Hub function: info in src/cli/output.ts is called from 9 files (high fan-in expected for this module type) — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed [info]
- Potentially unused export: detectBranchLifecycle in src/analyzers/branch-work-filter.ts has no incoming calls — verify if still needed [info]
- Potentially unused export: resolveWorkedEpicTitlesForRange in src/cli/commands/prd-epic-resolver.ts has no incoming calls — verify if still needed [info]
- Zone "unit" has files across 11 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (unit)
- Before any new files are added: inspect the 11 files in this zone, identify the dominant shared responsibility, and assign a semantic name. The current ID 'analyzers-2' is an artefact of Louvain ordering and provides zero discoverability. Renaming costs one config edit; deferring makes every future zone reference ambiguous (corroborated by findings 6, 7, 10). [warning] (analyzers-2)
- ... and 4 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
